evidence: The paper explicitly uses "random survival forest (RSF) approach" to rank pathways and genes by "feature importance (RSF variable importance, p-value < 0.05)." Based on this ranking, they select pathways "with the greatest bearing on predictive power" and construct a final biomarker panel from the "top 40 IPMGs," stating that "the model taking the expression of the top 40 IPMGs as input achieved the highest 88.91% accuracy." Their main conclusion is that "the expression of the 40 IPMGs can serve as a set of pan-cancer prognostic biomarkers for immunotherapies." This relies directly on the ranking being correct to identify which genes should be included in the panel.
